untranslatable_reason: Megaphone pattern requires detection of two distinct diverging downward-sloping trend lines with at least two touches each. This requires trend line fitting, slope comparison, and multi-point touch validation which cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators (price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, Volume, Returns, Bar Properties). The pattern is fundamentally a visual/structural chart pattern that requires geometric analysis beyond the scope of single-bar indicator comparisons.
translation_notes: Signal describes a complex chart pattern (Megaphone) with specific geometric properties: two down-sloping diverging trend lines, broadening formation, downward tilt, and multi-touch confirmation. No combination of the allowed indicators can reliably detect this pattern. Bollinger Bands were used as a crude proxy for oscillation between boundaries, but this does not capture the trend line divergence or slope requirements. A candlestick pattern recognition or trend line detection module would be required for proper implementation.
